figures.gloss: Revered not as an evil adversary but as the ageless Principle of Isolate Intelligence. Set is considered a sentient, non-corporeal entity who deliberately altered human evolution to grant self-awareness and separate humanity from the blind mechanics of nature.
figures.facet: Set is not a rebel against a moral god, but an active rebel against mindless, mechanistic cosmic stasis, valuing conscious evolution and absolute individuality above all.
figures.provenance: Michael Aquino, 'The Book of Coming Forth by Night' (1975).

figures.gloss: The central magical and philosophical axiom of the Temple, translated from the Egyptian verb meaning 'I have come into being.' It represents the deliberate, continuous process of evolving one's isolate intelligence to higher states of self-deification.
figures.facet: Unlike traditional mystical paths that seek the dissolution of the ego into a universal oneness, Xeper demands the absolute, eternal crystallization and expansion of the individual will.
figures.provenance: Derived from the Egyptian scarab hieroglyph (kheper); formalized as a magical mandate in 'The Book of Coming Forth by Night'.

figures.gloss: The literal, non-natural evolutionary leap bestowed upon humanity by Set. It is the capacity for higher consciousness, subjective awareness, and the magical will, fundamentally alienating humans from the deterministic 'Objective Universe.'
figures.facet: It posits that human sentience is a deliberate, unnatural injection of subjective potential by an external intelligence, not a blind byproduct of biological evolution.

figures.gloss: The personification of the Objective Universe in its most mindless, entropic state. In Setian cosmology, it is the force of universal stasis, conformity, and meaninglessness that threatens to extinguish the isolate flame of human consciousness.
figures.facet: Evil is completely redefined: it is not a moral failing or transgression against divine law, but the surrender of conscious, isolate intelligence to the mechanical, unconscious forces of the natural world.
figures.provenance: Don Webb, 'The Seven Faces of Darkness' (1996); adapted from the Egyptian serpent of chaos.

figures.gloss: The formalized practice of causing change to occur in the subjective universe (the mind) in accordance with the will, which in turn necessitates a proportional change in the objective universe through probabilistic manipulation.
figures.facet: It relies purely on the focused, isolate will of the magician to alter reality's probabilistic outcomes, strictly rejecting submissive prayer to gods or reliance on blind cosmic currents.
figures.provenance: Michael Aquino, 'Black Magic'; adapting and intellectually refining Anton LaVey's operational concepts.

figures.gloss: The highest attainable initiator state within the Temple, recognized only when a Setian utters a new 'Word'—a novel magical paradigm or concept that fundamentally reshapes the spiritual and intellectual landscape of humanity.
figures.facet: True magical mastery is proven not by localized spellcraft or hierarchical rank, but by generating a universe-altering conceptual framework that independently survives and influences the world.
figures.provenance: Temple of Set initiatory structure, philosophically descended from Aleister Crowley's A.'.A.'. degrees but tailored to Left-Hand Path ontology.

figures.gloss: The principal founder of the Temple of Set and the Magus of the Word of Xeper. A former US Army intelligence officer, he formalized Left-Hand Path occultism into a rigorous, Platonically inspired ontological framework.
figures.facet: Aquino brought an unprecedented academic, philosophical, and military precision to Left-Hand Path occultism, severing its historical reliance on mere anti-Christian theatricality to build a standalone metaphysical system.
figures.provenance: Historical founding of the Temple of Set (1975); author of 'The Church of Satan' (1983) and 'MindStar' (2003).

edges.note: Intra-tradition: Set is the literal embodiment and teacher of Xeper, the principle of coming into being.
Intra-tradition: The Gift of Set is the specific mechanism by which Set passed isolate intelligence to humanity.
Intra-tradition: Set represents dynamic, isolate consciousness, while Apep represents the mindless, entropic stasis of the objective universe.
Intra-tradition: The state of Magus is realized by uttering a conceptual Word, of which Xeper was the foundational utterance for the Temple.
Intra-tradition: Greater Black Magic is the active operational practice used to achieve the evolutionary state of Xeper.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: In Setian theology, Osiris explicitly represents the mindless stasis and deterministic cycles of nature, which Set actively defies and murders.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both are entities who deliberately intervened in human evolution to gift humanity with the spark of higher consciousness and intellect.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both are nocturnal, adversarial figures associated with the manipulation of fate, subjective reality, and testing humanity's individual volition.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both represent the forces of illusion and entrapment that keep human consciousness asleep and bound to mindless, cyclic existence.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both signify the ignorant, blind mechanical order of the physical universe, viewed by the tradition as a spiritual obstacle to true divine consciousness.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both traditions view the ultimate spiritual culmination not as merging with the universe, but as an eternal, autonomous, and perfected isolate state.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: A radical philosophical inversion; where Buddhism seeks the realization of no-self (Anatman), Setianism seeks the absolute hardening and apotheosis of the individual ego.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both identify a fragment of non-natural, transcendent consciousness residing within humans, profoundly alien to the physical cosmos.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: A structural parallel where the practitioner maps and manipulates the fluid subjective realm (Nagual) to force necessary changes in the fixed objective world (Tonal).
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both were charismatic synthesizers who formalized disparate esoteric and adversarial currents into highly rigorous, intellectually complex dualistic religions.
Cross-tradition GOLD SEAM: Both act as cosmic disruptors who break the static, pre-ordained order of the universe, introducing the necessary chaos that allows for individuation.

dossier.essence: A Left-Hand Path initiatory order founded in the United States in 1975 by Michael Aquino and colleagues who broke from Anton LaVey's Church of Satan, rejecting LaVey's atheistic 'Satan as symbol' for the recognition of Set — the ancient Egyptian god reinterpreted as the timeless 'principle of isolate intelligence' — as a real, distinct entity. Its members pursue Xeper (Egyptian 'to come into being'), the deliberate, self-directed evolution of the individual psyche toward self-deification. It is a small, deliberately selective international order.
dossier.cosmos: Reality is split between the Objective Universe — the natural, deterministic, mechanical cosmos — and the Subjective Universe of individual consciousness. Set is the non-natural intelligence who gave humanity self-awareness, severing it from mindless nature (the 'Gift of Set'), while the entropic drift back toward stasis is figured as Apep. The goal is not dissolution into cosmic unity but the eternal crystallization and expansion of the sovereign self, whose psyche initiation seeks to render capable of surviving death; there is no imposed collective salvation or afterlife dogma.
dossier.practice: Members advance through a six-degree initiatory system organized in regional 'Pylons,' emphasizing rigorous reading, philosophy, and individual 'Work' rather than congregational worship. Ritual divides into Greater Black Magic (formal working upon the subjective universe to reshape probability) and Lesser Black Magic (psychological and rhetorical influence). Admission and advancement depend on demonstrated intellectual initiative, not mere membership.
dossier.canon: Aquino's revealed 'The Book of Coming Forth by Night' (1975) and his documentary institutional histories, supported by Don Webb's guides to the Left-Hand Path and Stephen E. Flowers' 'Lords of the Left-Hand Path' (1997).
dossier.reflects: The sacralization of the sovereign individual — a path that inverts mystical union, making the hardening and apotheosis of the isolate self, not its dissolution, the object of ultimate concern.
sources: Gareth Medway, 'Lure of the Sinister: The Unnatural History of Satanism' (2001)
Per Faxneld & Jesper Aa. Petersen (eds.), 'The Devil's Party: Satanism in Modernity' (2013)
Asbjorn Dyrendal, James R. Lewis & Jesper Aa. Petersen, 'The Invention of Satanism' (2016)
